result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, following the existing static-class style:

- **`EscapeField`** returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** walks the line field by field: a field starting with `"` is scanned until the closing un-doubled quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside treated as content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The empty line parses to one empty field, and a trailing comma produces a final empty field.

The project builds cleanly (0 warnings, 0 errors). I also verified behavior at runtime via a throwaway console project (deleted afterward, nothing added to `src`): 13 roundtrip cases all passed, including embedded commas, doubled quotes, CR/LF content, empty fields at every position, and the empty-list/empty-line special case from the spec.
